sections.title: Friendship Likeness
questions: I have significant time to invest in building new friendships right now.
I am looking for long-term friendships rather than casual acquaintances.
I am open to new connections.
I am comfortable with meeting more than just one match.
I am willing to make time for friendships and community.
I am in a place where I can be a reliable friend.
I am willing to be vulnerable and share personal information with new friends.
I have unresolved issues from past friendships that may affect new friendships.
I am ready to show up consistently for a new friend, even when it's inconvenient.

sections.title: Personality
questions: I am comfortable with friendships that have periods of less frequent contact.
I am willing to be the one who usually initiates plans and reaches out first.
I find it easy to be vulnerable with my friends.
I find it easy to hold space for my friends.
Past friends have told me I am passive-aggressive.
I have been told I am avoidant.
I would identify as a people-pleaser.
I am very independent.
It's hard for me to accept help.
I often need reassurance from friends that they value our friendship.
I prefer to keep some emotional distance, even with close friends.
I tend to reach out to friends frequently to maintain the connection.
I push friends away when I feel like we are getting too closed.
I feel secure in my friendships, even when we go a while without talking.
I view friends as family.
My partner(s) come(s) before my friends.
I tend to lean more right in politics.
I tend to lean more left in politics.
Politics matter in my friendships.
I prefer friends who have similar political beliefs as me.

sections.title: Rupture and Repair
questions: In conflict, I am willing to initiate repair first.
I tend to avoid conflict.
I find it easy to take responsibility for my part in conflict.
I tend to metaphorically sweep things under the rug.
I prefer to resolve disagreements immediately rather than taking time to cool off.
I prefer to address conflicts directly rather than letting issues face away.
I can forgive friends easily after they've hurt my feelings.
I hold onto hurt feelings for a long time, even after an apology.
After an argument, I need my friend to reach out first to reconcile.
I am comfortable apologizing first, even if I don't think I was entirely at fault.

questions.question: Cooking together or trying a new restaurant together
Video chat catch-up versus in-person hangouts
Sends reels versus writes me letters
A voice memo versus a yap session
Time alone to recharge versus receiving energy from hanging out
Seeing friend once a week versus every once in a while
I am more realistic/practical versus imaginative/creative
